title: Encoding and Gradient in Physics-Informed Networks: Tokenizer-Free Models and Controllable Explosion Optimization
description: We present an analysis of tokenizer-free language models focusing on byte-level (UTF-8) and Unicode BMP encoding schemes. The key finding is that Unicode BMP encoding reduces Chinese sequence length by 3x compared to UTF-8 bytes while preserving semantic completeness. We identify and analyze the collapse phenomenon in deep networks trained from scratch without pretraining, where both LSH-SFA and Transformer baselines exhibit training instability. Our analysis reveals that this is a shared limitation of fine-grained byte-level classification rather than an architecture-specific defect. We propose model capacity boundaries for different encoding schemes and demonstrate that Unicode BMP with 65,536 code points provides optimal balance between vocabulary size, Chinese efficiency, and tokenizer-free operation.
